meaning: n.火绒箱,易燃物,潜伏着暴乱或战争危险的人
examples: "Have you got my tinderbox?" asked the old witch.
You have got your money. Now, give me the tinderbox.
Then he took out his tinderbox and struck the flint.
History implies that high unemployment is not just an economic problem but also a political tinderbox.
Because official policy thwarted this natural process of renewal for many decades, Yellowstone became a tinderbox.
